summary.intent: Address Codex review pass-6 findings (5 issues) on PR #708 implementing DateTimePicker, then commit, push, reply/resolve threads, and re-trigger for pass 7.
summary.outcome: All 5 pass-6 findings fixed (fractional ISO value formatting, localized announcements via backend, roving tabindex for segments, CalendarSelectDate calendar projection, label `for` removal), 144 tests passing, all 10 xci-fast gates green, committed and pushed.

repo: DateTimePicker lives in crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/ with mod.rs, tests.rs, and a companion spec at spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md.
Time::to_iso8601() emits HH:MM:SS for whole seconds and HH:MM:SS.fff for fractional — fractional seconds must be surfaced in the ISO value output.
Segment keyboard navigation follows a roving tabindex pattern (mirroring TimeField): only the focused/first segment gets tabindex=0, all others get tabindex=-1.
Labels must not carry a `for` attribute pointing at the group element — accessibility requires pointing at a focusable target or omitting `for`.
CalendarSelectDate must reproject the selected date into the active calendar system before applying it.
Announcement localization (aria-live copy) is delegated to the backend, not formatted inline in the component.
The spec markdown uses fixed-width anatomy tables that require manual column-width alignment after edits.
CI gate is named xci-fast and runs 10 checks; snapshot tests auto-regenerate and diffs must be manually verified before acceptance.
Codex review is iterative (multi-pass); each pass can surface 3–6 findings; after 6 passes the finding count has not cleanly converged (6→6→5→3→4→5).

code.finding: format_iso8601 helper updated to emit fractional seconds when present, matching Time::to_iso8601() output format.
segment_attrs now implements roving tabindex: tabindex=0 only for focused/first segment, tabindex=-1 for all others — mirrors TimeField pattern.
label_attrs no longer emits a `for` attribute pointing at the group element.
CalendarSelectDate handler now reprojects the selected date into the active calendar before applying state.
format_announcement rewritten to delegate localization to the backend rather than formatting inline.
Tests added for roving tabindex behavior, fractional ISO output, and label-for removal; total test count grew from 141 to 144.

workflow: Snapshot tests must be reviewed by diffing before acceptance — automated regeneration can mask unintended regressions.
Spec markdown must be kept in sync with implementation changes each pass (tabindex semantics, attribute lists, helper notes).
Codex multi-pass review converges slowly on i18n/calendar surface; findings beyond stated acceptance criteria should trigger a scope conversation with the user.
Commit, reply/resolve, and re-trigger are a single atomic step per pass — partial completion leaves the review loop in an ambiguous state.

friction: Codex finding count is not converging (6→6→5→3→4→5 across passes), suggesting Codex keeps surfacing adjacent i18n/calendar surface beyond the original PR scope.
Spec anatomy table requires manual column-width measurement and alignment after every structural edit.
Multiple sequential Read→Edit cycles were needed to locate the correct spec lines (tabindex, helper notes, label sections), indicating the spec lacks stable anchors.
open_items: Pass 7 result is pending — the loop was re-triggered but verdict not yet received at transcript end.
Potential remaining Codex findings: full multi-char CJK day-period buffering and additional non-Gregorian calendar edge cases flagged as likely next surfaces.
Scope question deferred: whether to continue grinding to 👍 or cap at current state given findings exceed PR #292 acceptance criteria — user chose to continue but this may resurface.
